steps.title: Identify what the conclusion relies on
steps.content:
The conclusion states that Hikwama provided more specificity in its data.

The passage defines the detail rating with the phrase “reported data with more detail”; specificity in data is therefore determined by the detail rating.

This means the conclusion rests on Hikwama having a higher detail rating than Townsend.

steps.title: Find the direct contradiction
steps.content:
The matching choice directly contradicts the detail rating comparison that the conclusion assumes: it states that Hikwama had a lower detail rating than Townsend.

The other choices address traceability or Townsend's absolute ratings without contradicting the comparative claim; a remaining choice supports the conclusion instead.

B is correct.
